channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Somewhere in Japan is a dispenser where you can buy toy rack servers complete with cute Dell PowerEdge 2U servers
item.description: You can get toy rack servers, modeled after real products from Dell, Cisco, and A10 Networks, in Japanese vending machines
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Vance hopeful 'high-level' TikTok deal will be completed by early April
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Friday that he was hopeful a deal to keep TikTok operating in the U.S. will be wrapped up by the early April deadline.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Governments won’t like this: encrypted messaging between Android and iOS devices coming, says GSMA
item.description: Imagine a world of the near future where Android and Apple iOS users can message one another with the certainty that their communication is secured against eavesdropping by end-to-end encryption (E2EE).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Bay Area adds jobs in January while California hiring grinds to a halt
item.description: The Bay Area gained jobs in January, fueled by sturdy gains in the South Bay and East Bay, although the San Francisco metro region lost jobs.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: West Virginia football coach Rich Rodriguez isn’t here for locker room dances
item.description: From the White House to the local police department, TikTok trends are now part of the cultural conversation—for better or worse. But don’t expect to see the latest dance craze in the West Virginia football team’s locker room.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: IDC: 80% of companies plan to buy AI PCs this year
item.description: AI PCs could solve key issues organizations face when using cloud and data center AI instances, including cost, security, and privacy concerns, according to a new study by IDC Research. Nearly all organizations are already using or planning to use cloud-based AI platforms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Senate Banking panel advances stablecoin bill
item.description: The Senate Banking Committee voted Thursday to advanced legislation to create a regulatory framework for payment stablecoins.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Intel shares jump after naming chip industry veteran Tan CEO
item.description: Shares of Intel Corp. jumped the most in five years after the company named Lip-Bu Tan as its next chief executive officer, entrusting a former board member and semiconductor veteran with one of the toughest jobs in the chip industry.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: I’m a tech pro and these are my secrets to better flights and luxury travel
item.description: Here are some travel hacks to save money on your next vacation including setting up price alerts, remaining flexible and checking for 24-hour price drops.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability
item.description: This approach emphasizes the importance of integrating security measures into the software development lifecycle from the beginning. The post Creating a cybersecurity standard of care: The future of software liability first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI takes on rivals with new Responses API, Agents SDK
item.description: OpenAI’s new Responses API and an upgraded Agents SDK will help enterprises more easily build agents with advanced reasoning and multimodal capabilities, the company said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Laser-wielding robots are redefining farming
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New tools from OpenAI help companies create their own AI agents
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday launched Responses, a new api intended to eventually replace Assistants. The big draw? Responses provides a number of new tools that companies and organizations can use to create their own AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster
item.description: Rethinking the delivery process for new capabilities is essential to put cybersecurity and the warfighter front and center. The post How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Affordable Laptops and Data Transfer Options for a Retired Computer User
item.description: Discover the best laptops for retired computer users, including budget-friendly options from Acer, Lenovo, and Asus. Learn about affordable data transfer methods using cloud storage or external drives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
